A well known publisher hes expressed an interest in our planned publi-'
cation consisting of a compilation of articles written by various authore for:
use in our profession as a much needed reference. This interest was expressed
even though this publication may not be considered a profit item. While in ~—
Minneapolis you will be asked to review a suggested list of chapters for this.
book. and also estimate the number of copies your department would order. In
order to make this venture successful, it will be necessary to sell at least —

one thousand books.
I have had an opportunity to review a copy of the recent Rules and
Regulations for the Administration of Vocational Education. It is indeed
gratifying to note a number.of modifications dealing with teacher education
made since the earlier drafts of this document. We are indebted to Dr. W. Me.
Arnold, Assistant Commissioner of Vocational and Technical Education for his —
. consideration of suggestions from our teacher education organization.
In addition to the importent matters of business, I am certain we are
all looking forward to our Annual Breekfast and the MYSTERY SPEAKER. Charles
W. Hill, our president-elect, with the help of Milo Peterson, our secretery,
who is at the convention site, are making final arrangements for this meeting.
I shall be looking forward to seeing you in Minneapolis, December 7-11, .
and please send me any suggestions or ideag you may have in regard to the
AATEA's responsibility in strengthening teacher education now and in the
years ahead.
Dwight L. Kindschy
